figures.caption: Figure 3: The plots for input data rate, load interval, bandwidth, output drops versus time for a few data points from the CSV dataset.

Figure 6: The convergence of time-average active states y1ν and y2ℓ of AI critics evaluating tasks of (a) modality 1 and (b) modality 2, respectively, to close to their respective optimal values y∗1ν and y∗2ℓ, ν = 1, 2, . . . , C1, and ℓ = 1, 2, . . . , C2.

Table 5: Fault severity and cause analysis for critics enabled with large language models. The critics evaluate the responses of agents and send them feedback for improvement. They also classify the query as Non-critical or Critical and briefly summarize the fault cause. The critics also have access to network telemetry logs that were vector-embedded and stored in ChromaDB. Note that the large language models generated the text in the Cause Summary.
Table 4: Fault severity and cause analysis: The table presents the text queries classified as ‘Critical’ by different LLMs. The LLMs generated the text in the Cause Summary.
Figure 4: Federated multi-agent system’s block diagram: AI agents and critics coordinate with a central server to complete multimodal tasks.
Figure 1: Block diagram for essential components of AI agent-critic interaction. Models represent the foundation models; for example, large language models, large vision models, etc.

Figure 11: The UpSet plot illustrates the intersection of sets of faults classified as critical by various LLMs. The bars on the bottom left represent the number of critical faults classified by the LLMs. The bars above the single solid dot represent the number of faults classified as critical only by that large language model (a disjoint set). The connecting solid dots represent the common queries classified as critical by the connecting LLMs.
Table 3: Fault severity and cause analysis: The table presents the text queries classified as ‘Critical’ by different LLMs. The LLMs generated the text in the Cause Summary.

Figure 10: The proportion of critical and non-critical faults classified by various open-source large language models. The green slice represents the proportion of non-critical faults, and the exploded red slice represents the proportion of critical faults.

Table 2: Fault severity and cause analysis for agents enabled with large language models. The agents classify the query as Non-critical or Critical and briefly summarize the fault cause. The agents have access to network telemetry logs that were vector-embedded and stored in ChromaDB. Note that the large language models generated the text in the Cause Summary.

Figure 5: The convergence of time-average active states x1i and y2i of AI agents performing tasks of (a) modality 1 and (b) modality 2, respectively, to close to their respective optimal values x∗1i and x∗2i.
Figure 2: Density plots for input data rate, output data rate, bandwidth, and bytes sent for the CSV file (all rows) of the network telemetry dataset 4 of [15].
